title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 27 27

questions.question: Transform the letters in OVERMATCH: apply "shift each vowel forward by 3" to vowels and "shift each consonant backward by 3" to consonants. After transformation, how many letters lie between the third from the left and the fourth from the right of the new string?
questions.explanation: Original word: OVERMATCH O → R V → S E → H R → O M → J A → D T → Q C → Z H → E Transformed string: R S H O J D Q Z E Third from left = H Fourth from right = D Letters between H and D => O, J (2 letters)

questions.question: Compare the following two tax invoice records. Record 1: Invoice No.: INV-25-07198, GSTIN: 27ABCDE1234F1Z5, Buyer: Orion Tech Pvt Ltd, Amount: ₹58,320, Date: 31-08-2025 Record 2: Invoice: INV/25/07198, GSTIN: 27ABCDE1234F1Z6, Buyer: Orion Technologies Pvt. Ltd., Amount: ₹58,320.00, Date: 31/08/2025 Are these likely to be the same?
options: No, the invoice numbers are different
questions.explanation: Invoice number matches after punctuation normalization. Buyer name is an expanded variant; amount and date match after format normalization. GSTIN differs in the last character: F1Z5 vs F1Z6. GSTIN identifies the registered entity; records are not the same.

questions.question: Read the situation and choose the best judgment: On a crowded railway platform, you notice an unattended bag left near a pillar. No one claims it despite announcements for the next train. There are families and children nearby.
options: Open the bag carefully to check for an ID and call the owner.
Inform station staff or security at once and keep a safe distance, discouraging others from approaching until officials arrive.
Move the bag to an empty corner so it is away from the crowd.
Click photos and warn people on social media about a potential threat.
questions.explanation: Potential security risks must be handled by trained staff; maintaining distance reduces harm. Opening or moving the bag is unsafe and may trigger hazards. Posting on social media can cause panic and does not activate an official response. Alerting authorities promptly balances vigilance with public safety.

questions.question: Arrange the following words as per order in the dictionary 1. Prochronism 2. procrastinate 3. procuration 4. procession 5. procuratory 6. proclivity 7. procumbent
questions.explanation: The correct order is: 4. Procession 1. Prochronism 6. Proclivity 2. Procrastinate 7. Procumbent 3. Procuration 5. procuratory

questions.question: In each of the following questions, four words have been given out of which three are alike in some manner, while the fourth one is different. Choose out the odd one.
questions.explanation: Flip-flops is the odd one out because except for this the rest are all the different types of shoes. So, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: A woman introduces a man as "only brother of my daughter's paternal grandfather's daughter." How is the man related to the woman's father?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25528/image001.png) A woman introduces a man as "only brother of my daughter's paternal grandfather's daughter." ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25528/image002.png) That man is the son-in-law of woman's father.

questions.question: Select the number from among the given options that can replace the questions mark (?) in the following series: ⅖, ⅕, 3/10, ¾, ?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22806/image052.png) X ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22806/image060.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22806/image053.png) X ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22806/image061.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22806/image062.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22806/image054.png) X ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22806/image063.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22806/image055.png) X ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22806/image064.png) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22806/image056.png) Thus, the answer is option (a).

questions.question: 'A @ B' means 'A is the daughter of B' 'A % B' means 'A is the wife of B' 'A & B' means 'A is the brother of B' 'A # B' means 'A is the husband of B' As per which of the following expressions 'Q is the son-in-law of U'?
options: R & D @ Q # N @ K % U
Q & H % J & K # U @ N
U @ C & T % G & V # Q
J # Q @ V % E & U # T
questions.explanation: Option (a): R & D @ Q # N @ K % U R & D means 'R is the brother of D' D @ Q means 'D is the daughter of Q' Q # N means 'Q is the husband of N' N @ K means 'N is the daughter of K' K % U means 'K is the wife of U' ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25851/image013.png) Q is the son-in-law of U. Option (b): Q & H % J & K # U @ N Q & H means 'Q is the brother of H' H % J means 'H is the wife of J' J & K means 'J is the brother of K' K # U means 'K is the husband of U' U @ N means 'U is the daughter of N' ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25851/image014.png) Q is the brother-in-law of U. Option (c): U @ C & T % G & V # Q U @ C means 'U is the daughter of C' C & T means 'C is the brother of T' T % G means 'T is the wife of G' G & V means 'G is the brother of V' V # Q means 'V is the husband of Q' ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25851/image015.png) Q is the aunt of U. Option (d): J # Q @ V % E & U # T J # Q means 'J is the husband of Q' Q @ V means 'Q is the daughter of V' V % E means 'V is the wife of E' E & U means 'E is the brother of U' U # T means 'U is the husband of T' ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25851/image016.png) Q is the niece of U. Thus, the answer is option (a).

questions.explanation: P S V Y D G P S V Y D G P S V Y D G

Find the missing term. 7, 10, ?, 72, 291

In the following question, different letters stand for various symbols as indicated below: A : Addition B : Subtraction C : Multiplication D : Division E : Equal To F : Greater Than G : Less Than Select the option which shows the correct equation after letters are replaced with the respective symbols.
questions.explanation: Option (a), 43 A 120 D 5 B 2 C 8 G 37 43 + 120 ÷ 5 - 2 x 8 < 37 Here, L.H.S. > R. H. S. hence the option(a) is wrong Option (b), 43 C 120 D 5 B 2 A 8 E 37 43 x 120 ÷ 5 - 2 + 8 = 37 Here, L.H.S. ≠ R. H. S. Option (c), 43 A 120 B 5 C 2 D 8 F 370 43 + 120 - 5 x 2 ÷ 8 > 370 Here, L.H.S. < R. H. S. Option (d), 43 A 120 D 5 B 2 C 8 F 37 43 + 120 ÷ 5 - 2 x 8 > 37 43 + 24 - 16 > 37 51 > 37 Here, L.H.S. > R. H. S. Hence option(d) is true

questions.question: 'Metal' is related to 'Conductor' in the same way as 'Solid' is related to ______.
questions.explanation: Conductor is a property of metal and similarly definite shape is a property of solid.

questions.explanation: L M; I (j) K; G (hi) J; H (ijk) L; T (uvwx) Y. Similarly, R S; O (p) Q; B (cd) E; O (pqr) S; T (uvwx) Y

questions.question: Select the option in which the terms are related in the same way as are the terms of the given set. 520 : 1200 : 680
questions.explanation: First + third = second 312 + 255 = 567

questions.question: Select the correct combination of mathematical signs to sequentially place from left to right in the blanks below to balance the given equation. (26 _ 29) _ 11 _ 5 = 25
questions.explanation: When the combination of mathematical signs are sequentially placed from left to right in the blanks given below, then the equation will be: Option (a) (26 - 29) x 11 + 5 = 25 So, -3 x 11 + 5 = 25 So, -33 + 5 = 25 So, -28 ≠ 25 So, the equation is not balanced. Option (b) (26 + 29) - 11 x 5 = 25 So, 55 - 11 x 5 = 25 So, 55 - 55 = 25 So, 0 ≠ 25 So, the equation is not balanced. Option (c) (26 + 29) ÷ 11 x 5 = 25 So, 55 ÷ 11 x 5 = 25 So, 5 x 5 = 25 So, 25 = 25 So, the equation is balanced. Option (d) (26 - 29) ÷ 11 + 5 = 25 So, -3 ÷ 11 + 5 = 25 So, -0.27 + 5 = 25 So, 4.73 ≠ 25 So, the equation is not balanced.

questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All tea is coffee Some milk is tea No shake is coffee Conclusions: I. No milk is shake II. Some coffee is milk III. No tea is shake
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19864/image001.jpg) No milk is shake: Here, some milk is not shake. The milk which is not coffee can be shake. Thus, conclusion I is false. Some coffee is milk: All tea is coffee and some tea is milk. Thus, some coffee is milk. So, conclusion II is true. No tea is shake: All tea is coffee and no coffee is shake. Thus, no tea is shake. So, conclusion III is true.

questions.question: A word is represented by only one set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in two matrices given below. The columns and rows of Matrix I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of Matrix II are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from these matrices is represented first by its row and next by its column. e.g. 'A' can be represented by 01, 14 etc. and 'M' can be represented by 56, 68 etc. Similarly, you have to identify the set for the word 'PAGER'. Matrix I 01234 0PAGRZ 1GRZPA 2ZPAGR 3AGRZP 4RZPAG Matrix II 56789 5EMLNO 6LEOMN 7ONELM 8NOMEL 9MLNOE
questions.explanation: From the matrix, we have P → 00, 13, 21, 34, and 42 A → 01, 14, 22, 30 and 43 G → 02, 10, 23, 31 and 44 E → 55, 66, 77, 88 and 99 R → 03, 11, 24, 32 and 40 Only option 'c' has the codes of the letters in the same sequence.

questions.question: Find the first term of an arithmetic progression whose 8ᵗʰ and 13ᵗʰ terms are 18 and 25 respectively.
questions.explanation: 8ᵗʰ term = 18 a + 7d = 18 ... (1) 13ᵗʰ term = 25 a + 12d = 25 ... (2) After solving eq (1) and (2), We get d = 7/5 Now, from equation (1), a + 7 x (7/5) = 18 a = 18 - (49/5) a = (90 - 49)/5 = 41/5

questions.question: In a series of meetings conducted daily from 14th to 21st June 2021, the cities and dates were as follows: Lucknow - 21st June 2021, Kanpur - 20th June 2021, Prayagraj - 19th June 2021, Varanasi - 18th June 2021, Gorakhpur - 17th June 2021, Ayodhya - 16th June 2021, Faizabad - 15th June 2021, Sitapur - 14th June 2021. If the meeting held in Varanasi was on a Friday, then on which day of the week was the meeting held in Sitapur?
questions.explanation: Varanasi date = 18th June 2021 → Friday Now count backward to 14th June 2021 (Sitapur): 18 June → Friday 17 June → Thursday 16 June → Wednesday 15 June → Tuesday 14 June → Monday So, the meeting in Sitapur was held on Monday.

questions.question: Select the number that is related to the third number in the same way as the sixth number is related to the fifth number and the second number is related to the first number. 9 : 22 :: 12 : ? :: 21 : 46
questions.explanation: Rule: Multiply the number by 2 and then add 4. For 9: 9 X 2 = 18; 18 + 4 = 22. For 21: 21 X 2 = 42; 42 + 4 = 46 For 12: 12 X 2 = 24; 24 + 4 = 28. Thus, the answer is 28.

questions.question: Select the letter-cluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. ABDH, PQSW, GHJN, ?
questions.explanation: The pattern is: A+1= B B+2 =D D+4= H H+8 =P, P+1= Q Q+2= S S+4= W W+10 =G, G+1= H H+2= J J+4= N, Similarly, N+12 = Z Z+1= A A+2= C C+4= G

questions.question: The sentence below has a word in which the letters are jumble up. Rearrange the letters of that word, written in capital letters, to form the correct word. The team AEMD significant advancements in renewable energy, contributing to the development of technologies.
questions.explanation: Here, the sentence we get after rearranging the letters of the word is "The team MADE significant advancements in renewable energy, contributing to the development of technologies." Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: Who is the author of the book "Indira Gandhi and the Years That Transformed India", released in May 2025?
questions.explanation: Srinath Raghavan's latest book, Indira Gandhi and the Years That Transformed India, explores Indira Gandhi's political career in India's transformative 1970s, He shows how the 1970s were the hinge on which the history of the country turned - and how Indira Gandhi transformed the new postcolonial nation into the India of today. Notable books written by the other three authors: Ramachandra Guha (Historian, Biographer, Political Thinker) - India After Gandhi: The History of the World's Largest Democracy (2007) - Gandhi Before India (2013) - Gandhi: The Years That Changed the World, 1914-1948 (2018) - Democrats and Dissenters (2016) Shashi Tharoor (Politician, Diplomat, Author) - An Era of Darkness: The British Empire in India (2016) - Inglorious Empire: What the British Did to India (2017, UK edition of the above) - Why I Am a Hindu (2018) - Pax Indica: India and the World of the 21st Century (2012) - Ambedkar: A Life (2022) Nayantara Sahgal (Novelist, Essayist, Political Thinker - niece of Jawaharlal Nehru) - Rich Like Us (1985, won Sahitya Akademi Award) - Storm in Chandigarh (1969) - Plans for Departure (1985, shortlisted for the Commonwealth Writers' Prize) - Mistaken Identity (1988) - Indira Gandhi: Her Road to Power (1982) - Lesser Breeds (2003) - When the Moon Shines by Day (2017)

questions.question: Consider the following statements about Phylum Arthropoda: 1. Arthropods possess a chitinous exoskeleton. 2. Their body is segmented and often divided into head, thorax, and abdomen. 3. Arthropods have a closed circulatory system. 4. Jointed appendages are a characteristic feature of this phylum. Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: 1. Arthropods possess a chitinous exoskeleton (Correct) Arthropods are characterized by a hard, protective outer layer called an exoskeleton, which is primarily composed of chitin (a tough polysaccharide). Since this shell does not grow with the animal, arthropods must periodically shed it and grow a new one in a process called molting or ecdysis. 2. Their body is segmented and often divided into head, thorax, and abdomen (Correct) Arthropods have a segmented body (metameric segmentation). In many groups, such as insects, these segments are organized into three distinct regions known as tagmata: Head: Contains sensory organs (antennae, eyes) and mouthparts. Thorax: The middle section where legs and wings are typically attached. Abdomen: Contains the digestive, excretory, and reproductive organs. (Note: In some groups like crustaceans and spiders, the head and thorax are fused into a cephalothorax.) 3. Arthropods have a closed circulatory system (Incorrect) Arthropods have an open circulatory system. Instead of blood being confined to a closed network of vessels (like in humans), it flows into a central body cavity called the hemocoel. The fluid, known as hemolymph, directly bathes the internal organs to exchange nutrients and gases. 4. Jointed appendages are a characteristic feature of this phylum (Correct) The name "Arthropoda" literally means "jointed feet" (Greek: arthros = joint, podos = foot). These paired appendages are highly versatile and, depending on the species, are modified for walking, swimming, flying, sensing (antennae), or feeding (mouthparts).

questions.question: Who among the following viceroys suggested the Round Table Conference?
questions.explanation: In July 1930, the viceroy Lord Irwin suggested a round table conference (RTC) and reiterated the goal of dominion status. He also accepted the suggestion that Tej Bahadur Sapru and MR Jayakar be allowed to explore the possibility of peace between the Congress and the Government. Lord Chelmsford (1916-1921): - Oversaw the Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms, a significant step towards Indian self-governance. - His tenure was marked by the Rowlatt Act, which led to widespread protests and the Jallianwala Bagh massacre. Lord Linlithgow (1936-1943): - Viceroy during the crucial period leading up to Indian independence. - Faced the Quit India Movement and oversaw the transfer of power from British to Indian hands.

questions.question: During whose rule did Hieun-Tsang visit India?
questions.explanation: · Hieun-Tsang was a Chinese Buddhist monk and traveller who visited India between AD 627 and 643 during the reign of Harshavardhana of Pushyabhuti dynasty. · Megasthenes, an ancient Greek historian and diplomat, was sent by the Hellenistic king Seleucus I on embassies to the Mauryan emperor Chandragupta. · Faxian, a Chinese Buddhist monk, visited India occurred during the reign of Chandragupta II of Gupta dynasty. · Ibn Battuta, a Moroccan traveller, came to India during the reign of Muhammad bin-Tughlaq.

questions.question: The Fundamental Rights are enshrined in ______ of the Constitution of India.
questions.explanation: Part III of the Constitution outlines the Fundamental Rights, which are essential for the individual's freedom and dignity. These rights are designed to protect citizens from arbitrary actions by the state and ensure their freedom in various aspects of life. Part IV: Directive Principles of State Policy • Articles 36-51: These principles are guidelines for the state to promote social and economic welfare. While not justiciable (i.e., not enforceable by courts), they are essential for governance and aim to establish a just society. Part V: The Union • Articles 52-151: This part deals with the structure and functions of the central government, including the President, the Parliament (Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha), and the Council of Ministers. It also outlines the powers and responsibilities of each. Part VI: The States • Articles 152-237: Similar to Part V, this part deals with the structure and functions of the state governments. It covers the Governor, the State Legislature, and the distribution of powers between the states and the center. Here's a brief overview of the Fundamental Rights: Right to Equality (Articles 14-18): ○ Ensures equality before the law and prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth. ○ Includes the abolition of untouchability and titles. Right to Freedom (Articles 19-22): ○ Guarantees freedoms such as speech and expression, assembly, association, movement, residence, and profession. ○ Provides protection in respect of conviction for offenses and safeguards against arbitrary arrest and detention. Right against Exploitation (Articles 23-24): ○ Prohibits human trafficking, forced labor, and child labor. Right to Freedom of Religion (Articles 25-28): ○ Ensures freedom of conscience and the right to profess, practice, and propagate religion. ○ Includes the freedom to manage religious affairs. Cultural and Educational Rights (Articles 29-30): ○ Protects the rights of minorities to conserve their culture, language, and script. ○ Grants the right to establish and administer educational institutions. Right to Constitutional Remedies (Article 32): ○ Allows individuals to approach the Supreme Court for enforcement of their Fundamental Rights. ○ The Supreme Court has the power to issue writs for the enforcement of these rights.

questions.question: In the following question, a statement labeled Assertion (A) is given, followed by a statement labeled Reason (R). Assertion (A): The Permanent Settlement of 1793 created a class of hereditary zamindars. Reason (R): Zamindars were recognized as owners of land who had to pay a fixed revenue to the Company.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
questions.explanation: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, and R is the correct explanation of A. Explanation: Assertion (A) is true: The Permanent Settlement, introduced by Lord Cornwallis in Bengal, Bihar, and Odisha, established zamindars as the proprietors of the land, and their rights were made hereditary and transferable. Reason (R) is true: The core principle of the settlement was to recognize zamindars as the owners of the land in return for their commitment to pay a fixed amount of land revenue to the British East India Company. Relationship: The reason (R) is the direct cause of the assertion (A). The very act of recognizing zamindars as landowners with a permanent revenue obligation is what established them as a powerful, hereditary class. Thus, R correctly explains A.

questions.question: Article 361 of the Indian Constitution provides immunity to which of the following?
options: Judges of the Supreme Court from criminal proceedings
Members of Parliament for speeches in Parliament
President and Governors from criminal proceedings during their term
questions.explanation: Article 361 of the Indian Constitution provides specific "Protection of President and Governors" to ensure they can perform their constitutional duties without the threat of legal interference. Key Protections under Article 361: Immunity from Court Proceedings: The President or a Governor is not answerable to any court for the exercise and performance of the powers and duties of their office. Criminal Immunity: No criminal proceedings whatsoever can be instituted or continued against the President or a Governor in any court during their term of office. Immunity from Arrest: No process for the arrest or imprisonment of the President or a Governor can be issued from any court during their term. Civil Proceedings: Civil proceedings can be instituted against them during their term for personal acts, but only after giving a two-month notice in writing.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about Mission Vatsalya: 1. It is a centrally sponsored scheme that aims to secure a healthy and happy childhood for every child in India. 2. The mission primarily focuses on institutional care and seeks to phase out non-institutional care like sponsorship and foster care. 3. It is mandated to implement the Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015, and other related laws. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 3 are correct, while Statement 2 is incorrect. Statement 1 is correct: Mission Vatsalya is one of the pillars of 'Mission Shakti' under the Ministry of Women and Child Development. It is a centrally sponsored scheme whose objective is to ensure a safe and secure environment for every child. Statement 3 is correct: The scheme provides the framework and funding support to State Governments to effectively implement the provisions of the Juvenile Justice Act, 2015. Statement 2 is incorrect: This is the opposite of the mission's philosophy. The Juvenile Justice Act and Mission Vatsalya promote the principle of 'deinstitutionalization', emphasizing that non-institutional, family-based care (like adoption, foster care, and sponsorship) should be the preferred option, and institutional care should be the last resort.

questions.question: With reference to the Directive Principles related to economic and social justice, consider the following: i. Article 39(b) directs the State to ensure that the ownership and control of material resources are distributed to best serve the common good. ii. Article 40 directs the State to make effective provision for securing the right to work, within the limits of its economic capacity. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Only statement i is correct. Statement i is correct: Article 39(b) is a key Directive Principle of State Policy (DPSP) that reflects the socialist ideals of the Constitution. Statement ii is incorrect: Article 40 directs the state "to organise village panchayats." The provision for securing the "right to work" is mentioned in Article 41.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: Statement 1: The Himalayas are young fold mountains formed due to the collision of the Indian Plate with the Eurasian Plate. Statement 2: The Himalayas are one of the highest mountain ranges in the world. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options: Both Statement 1 and Statement 2 are correct
Neither Statement 1 nor Statement 2 is correct
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is True. Geologically, the Himalayas are classified as young fold mountains. "Young": They were formed relatively recently in Earth's history (starting about 40-50 million years ago) compared to older ranges like the Aravallis, which are billions of years old. "Fold": They were created through the process of folding, where the Earth's crust is pushed together and buckled. Formation: They formed at a convergent boundary where the northward-moving Indian Plate collided with the Eurasian Plate. The sedimentary rocks that had accumulated in the ancient Tethys Sea were compressed and uplifted to form these mountain ranges. This process is still ongoing, which is why the Himalayas continue to rise by about 1-2 cm every year. Statement 2 is True. The Himalayas are undisputed as the highest mountain range on Earth. Peak Altitude: They contain the world's highest peak, Mount Everest (8,848.86 m), along with more than 100 peaks that exceed 7,200 meters. The "Eight-Thousanders": Out of the 14 peaks in the world higher than 8,000 meters, 10 are located within the Himalayan range (the others are in the neighboring Karakoram range). Average Height: The average elevation of the Great Himalayan range is approximately 6,100 meters, which is significantly higher than other major ranges like the Andes (average ~4,000 m) or the Alps (average ~2,500 m).

questions.question: With reference to the regional kingdoms of early medieval India, consider the following statements: i. The Rashtrakuta dynasty, founded by Dantidurga, initially served as feudatories to the Chalukyas of Badami. ii. The tripartite struggle for control of Kannauj primarily involved the Rashtrakutas, the Palas, and the Cholas. iii. The Kailasanatha temple at Ellora is a renowned architectural achievement of the Rashtrakuta period. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements i and iii are correct. Statement i is correct: Dantidurga, the founder of the Rashtrakuta Empire, was initially a feudatory of the Chalukyas of Badami, whom he overthrew to establish his dynasty's rule. Statement iii is correct: The magnificent monolithic rock-cut Kailasanatha temple (Cave 16) at Ellora was commissioned by the Rashtrakuta king Krishna I. Statement ii is incorrect: The tripartite struggle for Kannauj was fought between the Rashtrakutas, the Palas of Bengal, and the Gurjara-Pratiharas of western India, not the Cholas.

questions.question: According to the Socio Economic and Caste Census, 2011, the percentage of rural households that pay income tax to the government is
questions.explanation: 4.6 per cent of all rural households in the country paid income tax while such households with salaried income are close to 10 per cent as per the first Socio Economic and Caste Census released. The percentage of Scheduled Caste (SC) households paying income tax was 3.49 per cent while Scheduled Tribe (ST) tax-paying rural households were mere 3.34 per cent, the Socio Economic and Caste Census 2011 said.

questions.question: Consider the following about hockey and choose the correct option. I. The striking circle in hockey has a radius of 16 yards (14.63 m). II. Each team fields 12 players at a time. III. A penalty corner is awarded for defensive fouls inside the circle.
questions.explanation: Statements I and III are correct, while Statement II is incorrect. Statement I is correct: The striking circle (or "D") in field hockey is a semi-circle with a radius of 16 yards (14.63 meters) from the center of the goal. An attacking team can only score a goal if the ball is played from within this circle. Statement III is correct: A penalty corner is awarded to the attacking team for a foul committed by the defending team inside their own striking circle. Statement II is incorrect: A hockey team fields 11 players at a time (10 outfield players and 1 goalkeeper).

questions.question: Which of the following team won the 42nd Indian Oil Servo Surjit Hockey Trophy held at the Olympian Surjit Hockey Stadium in Jalandhar, in November 2025?
questions.explanation: Indian Oil Mumbai clinched the 42nd Indian Oil Servo Surjit Hockey Trophy for the third consecutive time, and seventh time overall, by defeating Indian Railways Delhi 2-1 in the finals held at the Olympian Surjit Hockey Stadium in Jalandhar. The winning team scored both goals through penalty corners, whereas Indian Railways managed to score a field goal. The winning team was awarded the winner's trophy along with a cash prize of Rs. 5.51 lakh, while the Indian Railways team was awarded the runner-up trophy along with a cash prize of Rs. 2.50 lakh. Punjab Finance Minister Harpal Singh Cheema, who was the Chief Guest, appreciated the players and announced Rs. 25 lakh to Surjit Hockey Society. Talwinder Singh of the Indian Oil team was declared the best player of the tournament, while Punjab Police team Captain Olympian Harmanpreet Singh was declared the best scorer of the tournament and awarded with a cash prize of Rs. 51,000.

questions.question: Consider the following about Mohiniyattam dance and choose the correct option. I. It originated in Kerala and is traditionally performed by women. II. Its repertoire includes items like Chollukettu, Padam, and Tillana. III. It is based entirely on Kathakali-style martial expressions.
questions.explanation: Statements I and II are correct. Statement I is correct: Mohiniyattam is one of the eight major classical dance forms of India and it originated in the state of Kerala. It is a graceful, feminine (lasya) style of dance, traditionally performed as a solo dance by women. Statement II is correct: The repertoire of Mohiniyattam follows a structure similar to other classical dances. It typically begins with an invocatory piece called Cholkettu and includes expressive pieces like Padam (portraying love or devotion) and a concluding rhythmic piece like Tillana. Statement III is incorrect: Mohiniyattam is characterized by gentle, graceful, and fluid movements. It is not based on the martial, masculine (tandava) expressions that are a feature of Kathakali. While both are from Kerala, their styles of expression are distinct.

questions.question: 'Tawi River' is a tributary of _______.
questions.explanation: The Tawi River runs across the city of Jammu. It is regarded as a very sacrosanct river like the majority of other rivers in the country. It is a principal tributary of the Chenab River and flows through its left banks. The source of the Tawi River is closely located to the Kali Kundi glacier.

questions.question: What is the common name for the element with the chemical symbol "Pb"?
questions.explanation: The chemical symbol "Pb" corresponds to the element lead. Lead is a heavy metal with atomic number 82. It is a bluish-gray metal that has been used for various purposes throughout history, such as in construction, pipes, batteries, and as an additive in gasoline. However, due to its toxic nature, lead usage in many consumer products has been reduced or eliminated to minimize health risks. The symbol "Pb" comes from the Latin word for lead, which is "plumbum."

questions.question: According to Koeppen's scheme, which type of climate is denoted by the letter 'E' in India?
questions.explanation: The Kppen climate classification scheme divides climates into five main climate groups: A (tropical), B (arid), C (temperate), D (continental), and E (polar). Group E: Polar and alpine climates This type of climate has every month of the year with an average temperature below 10 C (50 F). Based on this classification Indian climate can be divided into following types: Type of Climate and its Areas Amw - Monsoon with short dry season - West coast of India south of Goa As - Monsoon with dry summer -Coromandel coast of Tamil Nadu Aw - Tropical savannah - Most of the Peninsular plateaus, south of the Tropic of Cancer Bwhw - Semi-arid steppe climate - North-western Gujarat, some parts of western Rajasthan and Punjab Bwhw - Hot desert - Extreme western Rajasthan Cwg - Monsoon with dry winter - Ganga plain, eastern Rajasthan, northern Madhya Pradesh, most of North-east India Dfc - Cold humid winter with short summer - Arunachal Pradesh E - Polar type- Jammu and Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand(Statement III is correct)

questions.question: Identify the correct statement from below. A. Jhijhiya is a famous folk dance of Bihar and is performed in the mythological town of Mithila. B. This dance is performed by women only.
questions.explanation: Jhijhiya is a famous folk dance of Bihar and is performed in the mythological town of Mithila. This dance is performed by women only. The whole Navaratri 'nine nights' festival is celebrated to worship the three forms of Lord Lakshmi, Parvati, and Saraswati. Further Insight: • Dances of Bihar ○ Jhijhian - Performed to invoke rain during droughts. ○ Jat-Jatin - Performed by rural folk, especially in the Mithila region, representing love stories. ○ Bidesia - Folk theatre dance popular in Bhojpuri-speaking areas. ○ Sohar - Performed on the occasion of the birth of a child. ○ Paika Dance - Martial dance, performed by the Paika community of Bihar. ○ Kajari

questions.question: In the Vijayanagar Empire, the Ayagar System primarily referred to:
options: A system of village servants receiving land grants or income for performing specific services
A military administrative system assigning land to warriors in return for service
A judicial system managed by temple authorities at the village level
A revenue assessment method based on land fertility and crop type
questions.explanation: The Ayagar System involved village functionaries such as headmen, accountants, watchmen and artisans who were compensated through land grants or income from land for their services. The ayagars were village servants or functionaries and constituted of groups of families. These were headmen (reddi or gauda, maniyam), accountant (karnam senabhova) and watchmen (talaiyari). They were given a portion of or plot in a village. Sometimes they had to pay a fixed rent, but generally these plots were manya or tax-free as no regular customary tax was imposed on their agricultural income. In exceptional cases, direct payments in kind were made for services performed by village functionaries. Other village servants who performed essential services and skills for the village community were also paid by assigning plots of land (like washerman and priest). The village servants who provided ordinary goods and services were leather workers whose products included leather bag used in lift-irrigation devices (kiapila or mohte), potter, blacksmith, carpenter, waterman (niranikkar: who looked after the maintenance of irrigation channels and supervised bankers and money-lenders). The distinguishing feature of the ayagar system is that special allocation of income from land and specific cash payments were for the first time provided to village servants holding a particular office.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes revenue receipts of the government?
options: Receipts that lead to creation of public assets
Recurring incomes that neither create liabilities nor reduce government assets
Receipts that are non-recurring and create liabilities
Receipts obtained mainly through borrowings and disinvestment
questions.explanation: Revenue receipts are: · Recurring in nature · Earned during a financial year · Do not create liabilities (unlike borrowings) · Do not reduce assets (unlike disinvestment) Further insights · Revenue receipts finance the day-to-day functioning of the government. · They consist of tax and non-tax revenues. · A rise in revenue receipts improves fiscal discipline. · Revenue receipts exclude loans and asset sales. · Revenue deficit reflects weakness in revenue receipt mobilization.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: I. India holds the record for the most gold medals in Olympic hockey, all of which were won during the era of the unified Indian team. II. The Indian men's hockey team ended a 41-year medal drought by winning the bronze medal at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Both statements I and II are correct. Statement I is correct: India has won a total of 8 gold medals in men's Olympic field hockey, which is the most by any country. Statement II is correct: Before the Tokyo 2020 Olympics (held in 2021), India's last Olympic medal in hockey was a gold at the 1980 Moscow Olympics. The bronze medal win in 2021 ended a 41-year gap.

questions.question: Which of the following will be co-hosting countries for the next Men's Hockey World Cup scheduled from August 14-30, 2026?
questions.explanation: The next Men's Hockey World Cup will be held in Belgium and the Netherlands from August 14-30, 2026. Indian men's hockey team beat South Korea 4-1 in Rajgir, Bihar to become the Asia Cup champions and qualify for the next year's World Cup. India was led by Dilpreet Singh, who scored in the 28th and 45th minutes. Sukhjeet Singh opened the scorecard in the first minute, while Amit Rohidas helped India extend the lead in the 50th minute. Meanwhile, defending champions South Korea got their only goal through Dain Son in the 51st minute. · India remained unbeaten throughout the tournament, with five wins and a draw. The Harmanpreet Singh-led side won all their three pool matches. In the Super 4s, they beat Malaysia and China 4-1 and 7-0 respectively after playing out a 2-2 draw against South Korea. · With the triumph, India now has four Asia Cup titles. They had previously won in 2003, 2007 and 2017. South Korea have won the tournament five times in 1994, 1999, 2009, 2013 and 2022.

questions.question: With reference to constitutional provisions, consider the following pairs: 1. Article 110 : Definition of Money Bills 2. Article 112 : Annual Financial Statement (Budget) 3. Article 360 : Provisions for Joint Sitting of Parliament Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Pair 1 is correct: Article 110 of the Indian Constitution provides the definition of a Money Bill. Pair 2 is correct: Article 112 deals with the Annual Financial Statement, which is commonly referred to as the Union Budget. Pair 3 is incorrect: Article 360 contains provisions related to a Financial Emergency. The provision for a Joint Sitting of Parliament is detailed in Article 108.

questions.question: Determine resistance value of an appliance operating at 440 V and consuming a power of 1500 Watts.
questions.explanation: Power equation: P= V²/ R; P- Power consumption, V= operating voltage, R- Resistance According to the question, P= V²/ R or 1500= (440 x 440)/ R Or R= 193600/ 1500 Or R= 129 ohms

questions.question: There is certain number of four different fruits in a basket for Rs. 1450. The respective ratio of apples, mango, oranges and water melon in a basket is 3 ∶ 2 ∶ 4 ∶ 1. The price of an orange is 80% less than price of a mango and price of a watermelon is 20% more than price of a Mango. The price of an apple is 40% less than price of a mango. Find the total number of fruits in a basket if price of a Mango is equal to total number of fruits.
questions.explanation: " Let number of apples, mangoes, oranges and water melon be 3a, 2a, 4a, a respectively Let price of a mango be Rs. M Now, Price of orange = M – {M × (80/100)} = M/5 Price of watermelon = M + {M × (20/100)} = 6M/5 Price of apple = M – {M × (40/100)} = 3M/5 According to question, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/stubeimg/SSCT-MML-005070.png) {(3M/5) × 3a} + (M × 2a) + {(M/5) × 4a} + {(6M/5) × a} = 1450 9Ma + 10Ma + 4Ma + 6Ma = 1450 × 5 M × a = 250 Given, M = (3a + 2a + 4a + a) M = 10a Now, we have 10a× a = 250 a = 5 Total number of fruits = 3a + 2a = 4a + a = 10a = 10 × 5 = 50 "

questions.question: There are four distinct numbers- 19, P, Q, R such that the HCF of each pair of numbers (19, P), (19, Q), and (19, R) is 19 where P, Q, R, are each less than 80 and P < Q < R. What is the value of (R - P)/Q?
questions.explanation: Given that HCF of each pair of numbers (19, P), (19, Q), and (19, R) is 19, where P, Q, and R are each less than 80. So, the only possible values of P, Q, and R are 38, 57, and 76 respectively as P < Q < R. Now, (R - P)/Q = (76 - 38)/57 = 38/57 = ⅔.

questions.question: In a circle with centre O, GH is a chord such that ∠OGH = 60°. If OH = 24 cm, then what is the length (in cm) of the chord GH?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23079/image014.jpg) Radius = OG = OH = 24 cm So, ∠OGH = ∠OHG = 60° Thus, ∠GOH = 60° Hence, ∆OGH is an equilateral triangle. Thus, OG = OH = GH = 24 cm

questions.question: A square pyramid is placed in a cubical box having the edge of same length as that of the pyramid's base. Height of the square pyramid is thrice that of box. Find the ratio of volume of square pyramid to that of box?
questions.explanation: Let the side of cube be 'a'. Then the volume of cube, V₁ = a³ Volume of pyramid = (⅓) X Area of base x Height = (⅓) x a² x h Heights of square pyramid is thrice that of box i.e., h = 3a Therefore Volume of pyramid = (⅓) x a² x 3a V₂ = a³ Therefore, V₂/V₁ = a³/a³ = 1:1

questions.question: If A = 20²⁰⁰, B = 10⁴⁰⁰, and C = 6⁸⁰⁰, then which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p27230/image007.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p27230/image008.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p27230/image009.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p27230/image010.png)

questions.question: What is the value of: √2 x tan θ_________sec θ x √[(1 + cos 2θ)(1 + tan² θ)]
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er230928201est001.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er230928201est002.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er230928201est003.png) = sinθ

questions.question: The smallest number that can be added to 9999, so that 15 divides the resulting sum completely, is
questions.explanation: 9999/15 = (9900 + 99)/15 = 9900/15 + 99/15 = 9/15 Remainder when 9999 is divided by 15 = 9 Required number needed = 15 - 9 = 6 9999 + 6 = 10005 (divisible by 15).

questions.question: A man borrows Rs. 4500 on the condition that he will have to return this money in 10 Instalments of Rs. 500 per month. Find the rate of simple interest (per annum) .
questions.explanation: Amount Borrowed = Rs.4500 Amount paid = 500 ×10 = 5000 Interest Paid = 5000 – 4500 = 500 Now, considering time as 1 month, Principal will be = 4500 + 4000 + 3500 + 3000 + 2500 + 2000 + 1500 + 1000 + 500 = Rs.22500 So, P = Rs.22500, Interest = Rs.500, T = 1 month ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/stubeimg/e2c5a7be6f2eb062748193c36c49c010.png) "

questions.question: Find the third proportional to 72 and 54.
questions.explanation: " For third proportion b² = ac 54 × 54 = 72 × c C = 40.5 "

questions.question: The selling price of Y items is same as the cost price of 2(Y - 6) items. If the profit percent is 40%, then find the value of Y.
questions.explanation: Here, Y * SP = 2(Y - 6) * CP Then, SP/CP = (2Y - 12)/Y Profit% = 40 = ((2Y - 12)/Y - 1) * 100 Y = 20

questions.question: If two solid cubes of side 14 cm each are joined to form a cuboid, then what is the total surface area (in cm²) of the cuboid formed?
questions.explanation: After joining the two solid cubes, cuboid formed will look like: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21886/image001.png) Length of cuboid = 14 + 14 = 28 cm Breadth of cuboid = height of cuboid = 14 cm Total surface area of cuboid = 2(length * breadth + breadth * height + height * length) = 2(28 * 14 + 14 * 14 + 14 * 28) = 1960 cm²

questions.question: The radius of two circles with centres X and Y are 12 cm and 6 cm, respectively. If a transverse common tangent MN to both the circles which cuts the line joining the both centres X and Y at point O such that MO = 16 cm, then what is the distance between both centres of the circles if M is point on bigger circle and N is point on smaller circle?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p18763/image013.jpg) MO = 16 cm Since ∠XMO = ∠ONY = 90° By Pythagoras theorem, XO² = XM² + MO² => XO² = 12² + 16² => XO² = 144 + 256 = 400 => XO = 20 cm Since ∠XMO = ∠ONY = 90° And ∠MOX = ∠YON (transverse angle) So, ∠MXO = ∠OYN Hence, ∆XMO and ∆OYN are similar triangles. XM/YN = XO/YO => 12/6 = 20/YO => YO = 20/2 = 10 cm Hence, XY = XO + OY = 20 + 10 = 30 cm

questions.question: A sold an article at a loss of 40%. If the selling price had been increased by Rs. 4800, there would have been a gain of 10%. What was the cost price of that article?

A seller uses a faulty weighing machine that shows 800 gm of rice as 1000 gm. Due to this error, the seller earns a 30% profit while selling rice at cost price. If Rs. p is the cost price for 1000 gm of rice, what should the selling price be to maintain the same 30% profit?
questions.explanation: Actual cost of 800 g rice = p/1000 * 800 = Rs. 4p/5 Selling price = (4p/5) * 130/100 = Rs. 1.04p

questions.question: If x + 1_________x = √8, then find the value of x² + 1_________x² .
questions.explanation: Given, x + (1/x) = √8 Taking square of both sides, x² + 1/x² + 2 = 8 x² + 1/x² = 6

questions.question: The graph of 5y - x = 7 cuts the x - axis at point P(a, b). The value of (3b - 2a) is -
questions.explanation: Since, the graph of 5y - x = 7 cuts the x - axis at point P(a, b). So, y = 0 => b = 0 5*0 - x = 7 x = - 7 a = - 7 3b - 2a = 3*0 - 2*(-7) = 14

questions.question: What is the exterior angle of a regular octagon?
questions.explanation: Exterior angle of a regular octagon = 360°/8 = 45°

questions.question: Vikram and Neha started a partnership with initial investments in the ratio 3: 1. After 4 months, Neha added Rs. 8000 to her capital, and after 8 months from the starting, Vikram added Rs. 10000 to his capital. At the end of one year, their profits were divided in the ratio 11: 5. What is the difference between their initial investments?
questions.explanation: Let initial investments by Vikram and Neha be Rs. 3k and Rs. k, respectively. Vikram's time * weighted capital = 3k X 12 + 10000 X 4 = 36k + 40000 Neha's time * weighted capital = 1k X 12 + 8000 X 8 = 12k + 64000 Ratio of Vikram's profit to Neha's profit = 11/5 (36k + 40000)/(12k + 64 000) = 11/5 5(36k + 40000) = 11(12k + 64000) 180k + 200000 = 132k + 704000 48k = 504000 k = 10500 Hence, the required difference = 3k - 1k = 2 * 10500 = Rs. 21000

questions.question: Δ WYX is a right-angled triangle, right angle at Y. YZ is perpendicular to XW such that XZ = 5 cm, and ZW = 7 cm. The length (in cm) of XY lies between:
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p20923/image001.png) Since YZ is perpendicular to WX. So, YX² = XZ * XW XY² = 5 * 12 XY = √60 XY = 2√15 XY = 2 * 4 (taking approximate value) XY = 8 So, the value of XY lies between 7 and 9 cm.

questions.question: C and A together can complete a piece of work in 15 days. B alone can complete it in 30 days. If the work done by C in one day is equal to the work done by A and B together in 1 day. In how many days C alone can complete the same piece of work?
questions.explanation: According to question, C's efficiency = (A + B)'s efficiency.......... (i) Total work = LCM of 15 and 30 = 30 unit Efficiency of B = 30/30 = 1 unit Efficiency of (A + C) = 30/15 = 2 unit Efficiency of (A + B + C) = 3 unit........ (ii) Or 2 C's efficiency = 3 unit C's efficiency = 1.5 unit Time taken by C alone to complete the whole work = 30/1.5 = 20 days

questions.question: A vessel has a mixture of liquid K and liquid L in the ratio of 2: 1. When 9 litres of mixture is taken out and the remaining part of the vessel is filled with 3.6 litres of liquid L, the ratio of liquid K and liquid L becomes 5: 7. Find the quantity of liquid K in the vessel initially.
questions.explanation: Let, liquid K and liquid L in the mixture are 2x litres and x litres respectively. Quantity of liquid K taken out from the vessel = 9 * (⅔) = 6 litres Quantity of liquid L taken out from the vessel = 9 * (⅓) = 3 litres (2x - 6)/ (x - 3 + 3.6) = 5/7 => 14x - 42 = 5x + 3 => 9x = 45 => x = 5 litres Initially, liquid K in the vessel = 2x = 2 * 5 = 10 litres

questions.question: The average age of girls in a class is 42 and the average age of boys is 54. If the average of the total number of students (boys + girls) in the class is 47.5, then find the total number of girls in the class. The total number of students in the class is 48.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23488/image001.png) The total number of girls in the class = 48* 13/24 = 26

questions.question: Pritham's father is 6 years older than his mother, and the mother's age is now 5/2 times the present age of Pritham. At present, Pritham is 16 years old. What was the father's age when their son was born?
questions.explanation: Mother's present age = 16 * 5/2 = 40 years Father's present age = 40 + 6 = 46 years Hence, father's age when their son was born = 46 - 16 = 30 years

questions.question: A ladder is placed against a school's wall such that it just reaches the top of the school's wall. The foot of the ladder is at a distance of 70.3 m from the school's wall. The angle of elevation of the top of the school's wall from the base of the ladder is 60°. What is the length (in m) of the ladder?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p29911/image003.png) AB = Length of ladder BC = Distance between ladder's foot and school's wall = 70.3 m Cos 60˚ = BC/AB ½ = 70.3/AB AB = 140.6 m

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Tenebrous
questions.explanation: Tenebrous means dark, shadowy, or obscure; for example, "The tenebrous corridors of the ancient castle were frightening." Murky means dark and gloomy, especially due to thick mist; for example, "The divers could barely see in the murky water." Both words describe a state of darkness and lack of clarity, making murky the correct synonym. The other options - Radiant (shining brightly), Luminous (emitting light), and Cheerful (noticeably happy) are antonyms or unrelated in meaning. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person who can endure pain or hardship without showing feelings or complaining
questions.explanation: Pessimist: Someone who tends to see the worst aspect of things or believes that the worst will happen. Cynic: A person who believes that people are motivated purely by self-interest and generally distrusts human sincerity. Stoic: A person who remains calm and unemotional in the face of pain, hardship, or adversity, without complaint. Martyr: A person who suffers persecution or death for their beliefs, often religious, but not necessarily without expressing distress. The description "a person who can endure pain or hardship without showing feelings or complaining" aligns most closely with "stoic". Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The professor encouraged the students to question conventional theories and think ________ about existing paradigms.
questions.explanation: The sentence advocates independent and analytical thinking. The blank should support intellectual engagement. The correct option is "critically", which means analyzing and evaluating an issue carefully, not blindly accepting. The other options do not fit: "Passively" suggests no active thought. "Briefly" relates to duration, not quality of thought. "Obediently" implies submission, opposite of questioning. Thus, the correct sentence is: "The professor encouraged the students to question conventional theories and think critically about existing paradigms." Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given word. Supercilious
options: Having a haughty and disdainful attitude
Describing something that is soft and delicate
questions.explanation: Option (a) is the correct answer. "Supercilious" refers to an attitude of haughtiness, arrogance, or looking down upon others with disdain.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Before the auditors arrived for inspection, the finance manager had already prepared all the necessary financial statements and reports.
options: Before the auditors arrived, all the necessary financial statements and reports were prepared already by the finance manager.
Before the auditors arrived for inspection, all the necessary financial statements and reports had already been prepared by the finance manager.
All the necessary financial statements and reports were being prepared by the finance manager before the auditors arrived for the inspection.
Before the auditors arrived for inspection, the finance manager had been preparing all the necessary financial statements and reports.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive Voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of a "to be" form of verb ['been' for the given sentence] is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. In Passive Voice, the preposition "by" is used before the object. However, it is optional in some cases. The Active voice in this question is in Past Perfect Tense. Thus, the Passive voice will also be in Past Perfect Tense. The format in Active voice is: Subject + had + V3 + Object. Thus, the format in Passive voice will be: Object of the active voice (Subjective case) + had + been + V3 + by + Subject of the active voice (Objective case). For example: He had completed the task. (Active) The task had been completed by him. (Passive) Options (a) and (c) use incorrect tenses (Simple Past, Past Continuous). Option (d) is in the Past Perfect Continuous tense. Therefore, the correct answer is option (b): Before the auditors arrived for inspection, all the necessary financial statements and reports had already been prepared by the finance manager. Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: How does crowdfunding help researchers communicate with the public?
options: By publishing research papers in journals
By sharing updates and explanations of their work
By organizing international conferences in organizations
questions.explanation: It is mentioned in the second paragraph of the passage that "Researchers frequently share updates, videos, and explanations of their work with supporters, helping to make scientific knowledge more accessible and understandable." Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: The word "pursue" in the passage most nearly means:
questions.explanation: In the passage, the word "pursue" is used in the context of scientists working on new or innovative ideas. Here, it means to continue working on or carry forward an idea or project. Therefore, "follow" is the closest meaning, as it implies actively engaging in and developing something over time. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.question: Identify the grammatical function of the word "that" in the sentence: "..projects that might not easily receive traditional funding."
questions.explanation: In the phrase "..projects that might not easily receive traditional funding," the word "that" introduces a clause that provides additional information about the noun "projects." Since it connects the clause "that might not easily receive traditional funding" to the noun and helps describe it, "that" functions as a relative pronoun. It also acts as the subject of the clause, referring back to "projects." Demonstrative pronoun: Incorrect because "that" is not pointing to something specific independently. Conjunction: Incorrect as it is not simply joining clauses but describing a noun. Interjection: Incorrect because it is not expressing emotion. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following is the opposite of the word "unconventional" as used in the passage?
questions.explanation: In the passage, the word "unconventional" refers to ideas that are new, different, or not following established methods. Therefore, its opposite would be something that follows established practices or standard methods, which is "traditional." • Unusual: Incorrect because it is similar in meaning to "unconventional." • Experimental: Incorrect as it also suggests new or innovative approaches. • Irregular: Incorrect because it indicates lack of pattern, not necessarily adherence to tradition. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.question: Why do some research projects fail to receive traditional funding?
options: They are too expensive to conduct
They are conducted by inexperienced researchers
They are considered risky or unpredictable
questions.explanation: It is mentioned in the 1ˢᵗ paragraph of the passage that "One of the key advantages of crowdfunding is that it enables scientists to pursue unconventional or early-stage ideas that might not easily receive traditional funding. Many funding agencies tend to prioritize projects with predictable outcomes, while innovative or experimental ideas may be considered risky." The passage explains that traditional funding agencies often prioritize projects with predictable outcomes. As a result, innovative, experimental, or unconventional ideas may not receive funding because they are seen as risky or uncertain in terms of results. Therefore, option (d) accurately reflects the reason given in the passage. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Generally, Marathwada and parts of central Maharashtra receive rainfall a little late as this areas are known as rain-shadow regions.
options: have received rainfall a little late as this areas
received rainfall a little lately as these area
receive rainfall a little late as these areas
questions.explanation: The sentence talks about more than one area. Hence in the underlined part, the usage 'this areas' is incorrect because a plural modifier needs to be used to refer to a plural noun 'areas'. 'This' [singular] needs to be replaced with 'these' [plural] to rectify this error. Option (a) can be negated on this basis. Similarly, option (b) is incorrect because of the (incorrect) usage of 'these area'; here a singular noun 'area' has been referred to by a plural verb 'these'. Correct usage would be 'these areas'. Only option (c) has incorporated the required change and is grammatically correct and hence can replace the underlined part of the given sentence. The correct sentence would be - 'Generally, Marathwada and parts of central Maharashtra receive rainfall a little late as these areas are known as rain-shadow regions.' The answer is option (c).

questions.question: The figure of speech used in " Buzz! went the phone, interrupting the lesson" is:
questions.explanation: Onomatopoeia is the formation of a word from a sound associated with what is named. The word "Buzz!" imitates the sound the phone makes. Personification gives human qualities to non-human things, which is not happening here. Hyperbole is extreme exaggeration, but this is not about exaggeration; rather, it is a direct comparison. Metaphor compares two unlike things without using "like" or "as," so this is not a metaphor. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains a grammatical error. She was visibly angry on the unexpected delay in her flight, causing her to miss an important meeting.
questions.explanation: In segment (a), "on" is incorrect because it doesn't collocate correctly with "angry." The correct preposition should convey the emotion regarding the delay in her flight. "About" or "at" would be appropriate prepositions here, depending on whether the focus is on the emotion related to the delay ("angry about") or the situation itself ("angry at"). Hence, (a) is the correct answer. The corrected sentence: She was visibly angry at the unexpected delay in her flight, causing her to miss an important meeting.

questions.question: Identify the misspelt word.
questions.explanation: Among the options 'susecptible' is incorrectly spelt. The correct spelling is 'susceptible' (likely or liable to be influenced or harmed by a particular thing) Uncommunicative-unwilling to talk or impart information. Disillusionment-a feeling of disappointment resulting from the discovery that something is not as good as one believed it to be. Unreliability-the condition of being not reliable or untrustworthy. Hence, the correct answer is option (c).

questions.question: Choose the appropriate antonym of "Serpentine"?
questions.explanation: The word 'serpentine' is used to refer to something curvy or snake like. Hence, 'linear' is the most appropriate choice. 'Lethargic' refers to laziness. Ingenious - clever or innovative.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate antonym of Stifle:
questions.explanation: Stifle - Prevent or constrain (an activity or idea). So, 'encourage' can be its synonym. Enrage - Make (someone) very angry. Strut - Walk with a stiff, erect, and apparently arrogant or conceited gait. Compunction - A feeling of guilt or moral scruple that prevents or follows the doing of something bad. D is the right answer.

questions.question: What is a one-word substitute for a string of beads used to count prayers?
questions.explanation: 'Rosary' is the appropriate one-word substitute for the given group of words. The term 'rosary' describes a string of beads used to count prayers, especially in the Catholic tradition. 'Series' refers to a sequence of events, objects, or numbers. 'Shoal' refers to a large group of fish swimming together. 'Swarm' refers to a large group of insects, especially bees, flying together. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A) Despite their resilience and evolutionary longevity, horseshoe crabs face threats from habitat loss, overharvesting for bait in the fishing industry, and environmental changes, prompting concerted efforts to safeguard their populations and the interconnected ecosystems they contribute to sustaining. B) Horseshoe crabs, ancient marine arthropods with a lineage spanning hundreds of millions of years, play a crucial ecological role in coastal ecosystems. C) Horseshoe crabs, often referred to as "living fossils," possess a distinctive hard exoskeleton and are recognized for their remarkable blue blood, which contains a substance called hemocyanin crucial for detecting bacterial endotoxins. D) Their annual spawning events along coastal shores serve as a vital spectacle, attracting attention from both scientists and conservationists due to the significance of their eggs as a primary food source for migratory shorebirds.
questions.explanation: The correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph is BCDA. Sentence B introduces the horseshoe crabs and highlights their ancient lineage and importance in coastal ecosystems. Sentence C provides further details about horseshoe crabs, emphasising their unique characteristics such as their hard exoskeleton and the importance of their blue blood in detecting bacterial endotoxins. Sentence D elaborates on the annual spawning events of horseshoe crabs, highlighting their ecological significance as a food source for migratory shorebirds and drawing attention from scientists and conservationists. Sentence A concludes the paragraph by discussing the challenges faced by horseshoe crabs, despite their resilience, and the efforts being made to protect their populations and the ecosystems they are a part of. This sequence creates a coherent flow, starting with an introduction to horseshoe crabs, detailing their unique characteristics, discussing their significance during spawning events, and concluding with the challenges they face and the efforts towards conservation. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following question, the first and the last parts of the sentence/passage are numbered 1 and 6. The rest of the sentence/passage is split into four parts - P, Q, R, and S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find the most logical order of the parts between 1 and 6. 1: Volunteering offers numerous benefits, both to society and to the individual. P: It provides practical experience, develops new skills, and can enhance career prospects. Q: Those who avoid volunteering may miss opportunities for personal growth and social contribution. R: Volunteering also fosters empathy, teamwork, and a sense of community. S: In addition, it can reduce stress and increase overall happiness. 6: Therefore, actively participating in volunteer work can positively impact both the volunteer and society.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: b) PSRQ. Opening Statement (1): Introduces volunteering and its dual benefits. Sentence P: Explains career-related and skill-based advantages. Sentence S: Expands to personal well-being, including stress reduction and happiness. Sentence R: Highlights social benefits, such as empathy and teamwork. Sentence Q: Presents consequences of not volunteering, reinforcing the importance of the previous points. Closing Statement (6): Concludes logically, emphasizing positive impact on both the individual and society. Thus, the logical sequence is PSRQ.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom "Out at the elbows".
questions.explanation: The idiom 'out at the elbows' means ragged or impoverished. So, 'lacking money' is the right choice. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.question: Choose the sentence with correct collocation for the word 'permission'.
options: The students asked permission to leave the classroom early.
The students made permission to leave the classroom early.
The students built permission to leave the classroom early.
The students arranged permission to leave the classroom early.
questions.explanation: The correct collocation is "ask permission", which means to request approval before doing something. "Make permission", "build permission", and "arrange permission" are incorrect collocations in this context. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that uses a homonym of the underlined word: The leaves of the tree turned yellow during autumn.
options: The dry leaves fell quietly on the ground.
The leaves covered the path in the garden.
The wind scattered the leaves across the road.
The train leaves the station at six in the evening.
questions.explanation: In the question, "leaves" refers to the flat green parts of a tree or plant. A homonym is a word that has the same spelling or pronunciation but a different meaning. Option (d) uses "leaves" as a verb meaning to depart or go away from a place. Options (a), (b), and (c) all refer to the parts of a plant, which is the same meaning as in the question. Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. To give someone short shrift
options: To ignore someone or treat them with little attention
To praise someone with few words
To give someone too much freedom
questions.explanation: This idiom means to dismiss someone quickly and with little consideration or empathy. Usage: The editor gave the poorly written article short shrift. Hence, option (b) is the answer.

questions.question: In the following question, a blank is given indicating that something is missing. From the given options, choose the one that provides the correct word to be filled in the blank, thereby making the sentence grammatically and contextually correct. The conservation movement started bringing various species ______ around the turn of the century.
questions.explanation: The sentence talks of conservation movement; also, the blank requires an adverb at its place to modify the verb 'bringing'. The word 'back' (to return to an earlier or normal position or condition) fits correctly in the blank as its use indicates that movement was able to restore various species. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer. The phrase 'the turn of the century' is used to refer to the time when a particular century ends, and another begins. The adverbs 'off' and 'away' render the sentence meaningless, while the word 'rear' is a noun/adjective. Hence, options (b), (c) & (d) are eliminated.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Innate
questions.explanation: The word 'innate' means 'something that you were born with, not one you have learned'. 'Intrinsic' which means 'belonging naturally' is similar in meaning to 'innate'. 'Superficial' means 'not complete and involving only the most obvious things', 'acquired' means 'learned or developed (a skill, habit, or quality)' and 'extreme' means 'very great'. Hence, the answer is option a).
